snippets.title: with guarantees cleanup
snippets.code:
with open("example.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write("gen ai")
# f.closed is True here, even if write() had raised

# multiple managers in one statement
with open("example.txt") as src:
    print(src.read())   # gen ai
snippets.explanation: The file is closed when the block exits, whether it finished normally or raised.

snippets.title: A class-based context manager
snippets.code:
import time

class Timer:
    def __enter__(self):
        self.start = time.perf_counter()
        return self            # bound to the name after 'as'
    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):
        self.elapsed = time.perf_counter() - self.start
        return False           # let exceptions propagate

with Timer() as t:
    sum(range(100000))
print(round(t.elapsed, 4), "seconds")
snippets.explanation: __enter__ sets up and returns a value; __exit__ tears down and decides whether to suppress exceptions.

snippets.title: Generator-based manager with @contextmanager
snippets.code:
from contextlib import contextmanager

@contextmanager
def timer(label):
    import time
    start = time.perf_counter()
    try:
        yield                         # the with-block runs here
    finally:
        print(f"{label}: {time.perf_counter() - start:.4f}s")

with timer("call"):
    sum(range(100000))
snippets.explanation: Code before yield is setup; code in finally after yield is cleanup that always runs.

snippets.title: Scoping temporary state
snippets.explanation: A context manager cleanly applies and then guarantees restoration of temporary settings.
